'''Fanitesi Niu''' (born 11 August 2001) is a {{rlnt|Tonga}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a  {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He previously played for the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[NRL]].

==Background==
Niu was born on 11 August 2001 in [[Brisbane]], Queensland, Australia.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-05-08 |title=Tesi Niu |url=https://www.dolphinsnrl.com.au/teams/nrl-premiership/dolphins/tesi-niu/ |access-date=2024-08-06 |website=The Dolphins |language=en}}</ref> He was educated at [[Marsden State High School]] and played his junior rugby league for the Forest Lake Magpies.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dawson |first=Andrew |date=2018-10-12 |title=Tesi Niu – Thrilling Young Talent |url=https://southsloganmagpies.com.au/tesi-niu-thrilling-young-talent/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200327094603/https://southsloganmagpies.com.au/tesi-niu-thrilling-young-talent/ |archive-date=2020-03-27 |access-date=2024-08-06 |website=South Logan Magpies}}</ref> He is a first cousin of [[David Fifita (rugby league, born 2000)|David Fifita]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Ray|first=Jamie-Lee|date=31 May 2019|title=Tesi Niu draws Origin inspiration from Fifita|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2019/05/31/tesi-niu-draws-origin-inspiration-from-fifita/|publisher=QRL|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

Niu also represented the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|2018 Australian Schoolboys]].

==Playing career==
===2019===
Niu represented [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] making his international debut. He then played for Tonga 9s in the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/live-world-nines-png-out-to-heap-pain-on-beaten-kiwi-ferns/news-story/1b2c3df815703e59635881f548dc6ec3 |title=Thrills, spills and huge upsets: Every World Nines score and report |work=Fox Sports |date=19 October 2019}}</ref>

===Brisbane Broncos (2020–2022)===
On 4 June 2020, Niu made his NRL debut for the [[Brisbane Broncos]], coming off the interchange bench in a record 59–0 home defeat against the [[Sydney Roosters]]. On 27 June 2020, he made his starting debut at fullback against the [[Gold Coast Titans]].

Niu made a total of six appearances for Brisbane in the [[2020 NRL season]] after suffering a season-ending wrist injury in round 10, as the club finished last on the table and claimed its first-ever wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/24/brisbane-get-wooden-spoon-as-cowboys-finish-on-a-high/|title=Brisbane get wooden spoon as North Queensland finish on a high|website=www.nrl.com|date=24 September 2020}}</ref>

In round 16 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Niu scored two tries in a 26–18 victory over [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-07-04/nrl-south-sydney-beats-wests-tigers-brisbane-defeats-sharks/100233256/|title=South Sydney defeats Wests Tigers 38-22, Brisbane Broncos score 26-18 victory over Cronulla|website=www.abc.net.au|date=4 July 2021}}</ref>

Niu played a total of ten games for Brisbane in the [[2022 NRL season]] scoring three tries as the club finished 9th on the table and missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-brisbane-broncos-season-review/dag7jt0v67vvq6zzsmsxbqjl/|title=NRL 2022: Brisbane Broncos season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=14 September 2022 }}</ref> In the third group game at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]], Niu scored a [[hat-trick]] for Tonga in their 92–10 victory over the Cook Islands at the [[Riverside Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63448198|title=Tonga 92-10 Cook Islands: Tonga close Rugby League World Cup group in style|work=BBC Sport |date=30 October 2022 }}</ref>

=== Dolphins (2023–24) ===
[[File:Tesi Niu and Connelly Lemuelu.jpg|thumb|180px|Niu (left) and [[Connelly Lemuelu]] in 2024]]
Niu joined the newly-licensed [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] after he was granted an early release by Brisbane.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/tesi-niu-granted-early-release-broncos-set-join-dolphins-2023/ie7lfdb0wolfc9bsqch0mltk/|title=Tesi Niu granted early release from Broncos, joins Dolphins for 2023|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=5 January 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-01-04 |title=Niu departs Broncos |url=https://www.broncos.com.au/news/2023/01/04/niu-departs-broncos/ |access-date=2023-01-05 |website=Brisbane Broncos |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-01-05 |title=Niu kid on the block for Dolphins NRL debut |url=https://www.dolphinsnrl.com.au/news/2023/01/05/niu-kid-on-the-block-for-dolphins-nrl-debut/ |access-date=2023-01-05 |website=The Dolphins |language=en}}</ref> In round 1 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Niu made his club debut for the Dolphins in their inaugural game in the national competition, defeating the [[Sydney Roosters]] 28–18 at Suncorp Stadium. In round 3 of the [[2023 NRL season]], he became the first player in the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] NRL history to score a [[hat-trick]] when they defeated the [[Newcastle Knights]] 36–20.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-03-17/nrl-knights-dolphins-roosters-rabbitohs/102097528/|title=Sydney Roosters triumph over Rabbitohs 20-18 in tense NRL battle, Dolphins stay unbeaten with 36-20 win over Newcastle Knights|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 March 2023 }}</ref> In total, Niu played seventeen games and scored five tries for the Dolphins in 2023. His contract was extended until the end of 2024.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.dolphinsnrl.com.au/news/2023/06/30/niu2023/ | title=Niu earns contract extension | date=30 June 2023 }}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
In round 7 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Niu scored two tries in the Dolphins 44–16 victory over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-news-2024-parramatta-eels-vs-the-dolphins-live-stream-updates-start-time-teams-jesse-bromwich-dylan-brown/news-story/672e76464c7ec92abd251e7f31f1ca26/|title=Dolphins stun with nine-try onslaught to leave Eels shell-shocked in 'demoralising loss'|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> Following the end of the 2024 NRL season Niu turned out for feeder club [[Norths Devils]] and won the [[Queensland Cup]] with a 34-20 grand final victory over the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]]. Niu scored a double in the decider to earn the [[Duncan Hall|Duncan Hall medal]] for man of the match.<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2024/grand-final/game-1/ | title=Grand Final game | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240914033621/https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2024/grand-final/game-1/ | archive-date=2024-09-14}}</ref> Two weeks later he also helped the Devils win the [[NRL State Championship]] against NSW Cup premiers the [[Newtown Jets]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Jets v Devils |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-state-championship/2024/nrl-state-championship/jets-v-devils/ |access-date=2024-10-06 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}</ref> Niu confirmed that he would move to the [[Leigh Leopards]] at the start of the 2025 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2024-10-07 |title=Dolphins centre confirms new club |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/dolphins-centre-confirms-new-club-214946/ |access-date=2024-10-08 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>

===2025===
Niu made his club debut for Leigh in round 1 of the 2025 Super League season against rivals [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Leigh would win the match 1-0 in extra-time after the game finished 0-0 at the conclusion of 80 minutes.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2k5w971j35o|title=Leigh beat Wigan in extra time after historic 0-0 draw|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Niu played 26 games for Leigh in the 2025 Super League season including their semi-final loss against arch-rivals Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/3-key-areas-that-saw-leigh-leopards-miss-out-on-old-trafford-as-grand-final-opportunity-passed-up/|title=3 key areas that saw Leigh Leopards miss out on Old Trafford as Grand Final opportunity passed up|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
